name: Adult Autism Spectrum Quotient (AQ)
description: A 50-item self-administered instrument for measuring the degree to which an adult with normal intelligence has the traits associated with the autistic spectrum. The 50 questions are made up of 10 questions assessing five different areas: - social skill (items 1, 11, 13, 15, 22, 36, 44, 45, 47, 48); - attention switching (items 2, 4, 10, 16, 25, 32, 34, 37, 43, 46); - attention to detail (items 5, 6, 9, 12, 19, 23, 28, 29, 30, 49); - communication (items 7, 17, 18, 26, 27, 31, 33, 35, 38, 39); -
citation: Cohen S, Wheelwright S, Skinner R, Martin J, Clubley E (2001). The autism-spectrum quotient (AQ): evidence from Asperger syndrome/high-functioning autism, males and females, scientists and mathematicians. *J Autism Dev Disord.* 31(1): 5-17. Erratum in: *J Autism Dev Disord.* 2001 Dec;31(6):603. https://doi.org/10.1023/a:1005653411471 https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/11439754/

name: Alcohol Use Disorders Identification Test (AUDIT)
description: (The AUDIT-C is part of FROM-LP.) History and guide Auto-converted from CamCOPS; requires manual review.
citation: Fleming et al. (1991), http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/1743817; Saunders et al. (1993), http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/8329970, and others.

name: Ankylosing Spondylitis Disease Activity Score (ASDAS)
description: Four numerical rating scales rated 0-10 and a measure of inflammation (either C-reactive protein, CRP, or erythrocyte sedimentation rate, ESR). Each question is weighted: ASDAS-CRP = 0.12 × back pain + 0.06 × duration of morning stiffness + 0.11 × patient global + 0.07 × peripheral pain/swelling + 0.58 × ln(CRP + 1) ASDAS-ESR = 0.08 x back pain + 0.07 x duration of morning stiffness + 0.11 x patient global + 0.09 x peripheral pain/swelling + 0.29 x √(ESR) Intellectual property rights Auto-conver
citation: R, Sieper J, Dougados M, Davis J, Braun J, van der Linden S, van der Heijde D (2009). Development of an ASAS-endorsed disease activity score (ASDAS) in patients with ankylosing spondylitis. *Ann Rheum Dis.* 68: 18-24. https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/18625618

name: Assessment Patient Experience Questionnaire for CPFT Perinatal Services (APEQ-CPFT-Perinatal)
description: 6-item scale. Auto-converted from CamCOPS; requires manual review.

name: Bath Ankylosing Spondylitis Disease Activity Index (BASDAI)
description: Six numerical rating scales. A. Add scores for questions 1–4 B. Calculate the mean for questions 5 and 6 C. Add A and B (giving a score in the range 0-50) and divide by 5 The higher the BASDAI score, the more severe the patient’s disability due to their ankylosing spondylitis (AS). Intellectual property rights Auto-converted from CamCOPS; requires manual review.
citation: Garrett S, Jenkinson T, Kennedy LG, Whitelock H, Gaisford P, Calin A (1994). A new approach to defining disease status in ankylosing spondylitis: the Bath Ankylosing Spondylitis Disease Activity Index. *J Rheumatol* 21(12):2286-2291. https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/7699630/

name: Brief Psychiatric Rating Scale (BPRS)
description: 20-item scale. Auto-converted from CamCOPS; requires manual review.
citation: Overall & Gorham (1962), https://doi.org/10.2466/pr0.1962.10.3.799.

name: Brief Psychiatric Rating Scale, Expanded (BPRS-E)
description: 24-item scale. Auto-converted from CamCOPS; requires manual review.
citation: Lukoff et al. (1986), http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/3810065.

name: CAGE Questionnaire
description: The CAGE Questionnaire is a short screening tool for alcoholism. Its name is an acronym of its four questions (cut down, annoyed, guilty, eye-opener). History and guide Auto-converted from CamCOPS; requires manual review.
citation: Ewing (1984), http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/6471323.

name: Cambridge Behavioural Inventory, revised (CBI-R)
description: 45-item scale. Auto-converted from CamCOPS; requires manual review.
citation: Gray CH, Mason SL, Barker RA, Hodges JR (2008). The Cambridge Behavioural Inventory revised. Dementia & Neuropsychologia 2: 102–107. http://www.demneuropsy.com.br/imageBank/PDF/dnv02n02a04.pdf

name: Cardiff Anomalous Perceptions Scale (CAPS)
description: 32-item scale. Auto-converted from CamCOPS; requires manual review.
citation: Bell V, Halligan PW, Ellis HD (2006). The Cardiff Anomalous Perceptions Scale (CAPS): a new validated measure of anomalous perceptual experience. *Schizophrenia Bulletin* **32:** 366–377. http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/16237200

name: Center for Epidemiological Studies Depression Scale (CES-D, CESD)
description: A 20-item self-report scale for detecting depression. The CESD (1977) and CESD-R (2004) both have 20 items, but their content differs slightly. History and guide Auto-converted from CamCOPS; requires manual review.

name: Childhood Trauma Questionnaire, Short Form (CTQ-SF)
description: 28-item self-report scale relating to childhood adverse experiences including abuse. Intellectual property rights Auto-converted from CamCOPS; requires manual review.

name: Clinical Outcomes in Routine Evaluation 10 (CORE-10)
description: 10-item scale. Auto-converted from CamCOPS; requires manual review.
citation: Barkham et al. (2001), PMID `11393596 <https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/11393596>`_

name: Compulsive Exercise Test (CET)
description: A 24-item, 5-subscale self-report scale relating to compulsive exercise, applicable in the context of eating disorders. Each statement is rated from 0 (never true) to 5 (always true). History and guide Auto-converted from CamCOPS; requires manual review.
citation: Taranis L, Touyz S, Meyer C (2011). Disordered eating and exercise: development and preliminary validation of the compulsive exercise test (CET). *European Eating Disorders Review* 19: 256. https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/21584918/

name: Dementia Quality of Life measure (DEMQOL)
description: DEMQOL: 28-item interviewer-administered questionnaire answered by the person with dementia. History and guide Auto-converted from CamCOPS; requires manual review.
citation: Smith SC et al. (2005). Measurement of health-related quality of life for people with dementia: development of a new instrument (DEMQOL) and an evaluation of current methodology. Health Technology Assessment 9:1–93, iii–iv. http://www.pubmed.gov/15774233

name: Distress Thermometer
description: 36-item scale. Auto-converted from CamCOPS; requires manual review.
citation: Roth et al. (1998), http://www.pubmed.gov/9587123.

name: Eating Disorder Examination Questionnaire (EDE-Q 6.0)
description: The EDE-Q is the self-report questionnaire version of the Eating Disorder Examination (EDE) interview. There are 28 questions, and four subscales. To calculate a subscale score, calculate the arithmetic mean of the relevant rated items in the scale (listed below). The overall 'global' score is the arithmetic mean of the four subscales. In our implementation all applicable questions are mandatory so all items will have been rated. Subscale items (the numbers are the item number on the EDE-Q): Aut
citation: Fairburn CG, Cooper Z (1993). The Eating Disorder Examination (twelfth edition). In: Fairburn CG, Wilson GT (eds.).

name: Edinburgh Postnatal Depression Scale (EPDS)
description: 10-item scale. Auto-converted from CamCOPS; requires manual review.
citation: Cox JL, Holden JM, and Sagovsky R (1987). Detection of postnatal depression: Development of the 10-item Edinburgh Postnatal Depression Scale. British Journal of Psychiatry 150:782–786, http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/3651732.

name: Fast Alcohol Screening Test (FAST)
description: 4-item rapid alcohol screening test derived from the AUDIT. Items 1–3 use a frequency scale (0–4); item 4 uses a 3-option scale (0, 2, 4). Total score range 0–16; FAST-positive if total ≥3. Public domain (UK NHS/Public Health England).
citation: Hodgson, R., Alwyn, T., John, B., Thom, B., & Smith, A. (2002). The FAST Alcohol Screening Test. Alcohol and Alcoholism, 37(1), 61–66. https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/11825859/

name: Frontotemporal Dementia Rating Scale (FRS)
description: 30-item scale. Auto-converted from CamCOPS; requires manual review.
citation: Mioshi E, Hsieh S, Savage S, Hornberger M, Hodges JR (2010). Clinical staging and disease progression in frontotemporal dementia. Neurology 74: 1591.

name: Generalized Anxiety Disorder Assessment (GAD-7)
description: 7-item scale. Auto-converted from CamCOPS; requires manual review.
citation: See Spitzer et al. (2006) (http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/16717171) and the instructions (http://www.phqscreeners.com/instructions/instructions.pdf).

name: Geriatric Depression Scale, 15-item version (GDS-15)
description: Shortened version of the 30-item GDS. History and guide Auto-converted from CamCOPS; requires manual review.
citation: Brink TL, Yesavage JA, Lum O, Heersema P, Adey MB, Rose TL (1982). Screening tests for geriatric depression. *Clinical Gerontologist* 1: 37–­44.

name: Hamilton Depression Rating Scale (HDRS) / Hamilton Rating Scale for Depression (HRSD, HAM-D)
description: CamCOPS provides the full HAMD (= HRDS = HRSD) and the abbreviated HAMD-7. History and guide Auto-converted from CamCOPS; requires manual review.
citation: See Hamilton (1960), http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/14399272, and NINDS CDE on HAM-D, http://www.commondataelements.ninds.nih.gov/CRFs/Hamilton%20Depression%20Rating%20Scale%20(HDRS).pdf?CrfId=F0807.

name: Hamilton Rating Scale for Anxiety (HAM-A)
description: 14-item scale. Auto-converted from CamCOPS; requires manual review.
citation: See Hamilton (1959), http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/13638508, and NINDS CDE on HAM-A, http://www.commondataelements.ninds.nih.gov/CRFs/Hamilton%20Anxiety%20Rating%20Scale%20(HAM-A).pdf.

name: Health of the Nation Outcome Scale for working age adults (HoNOS)
description: 12-item scale. Auto-converted from CamCOPS; requires manual review.
citation: Wing et al. (1998), http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/9534825; Wing et al. (1999), http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/10616611.

name: Lynall M-E — IAM study — life events
description: Life events questionnaire for the Inflammation in Mind (IAM) study. A bespoke scale that is a version of the Brugha life events questionnaire, modified to include cumulative adult as well as recent stress. Intellectual property rights Auto-converted from CamCOPS; requires manual review.
citation: Brugha T, Bebbington P, Tennant C, Hurry J (1985). The List of Threatening Experiences: a subset of 12 life event categories with considerable long-term contextual threat. Psychological Medicine 15: 189-194. https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/3991833

name: Maternal Antenatal Attachment Scale (MAAS)
description: 19-item scale. Auto-converted from CamCOPS; requires manual review.

name: Michigan Alcohol Screening Test (MAST)
description: 24-item scale. Auto-converted from CamCOPS; requires manual review.
citation: Selzer (1971), http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/5565851.

name: Multidimensional Fatigue Inventory (MFI-20)
description: 20 questions rated on a 5-point scale (1 = "yes, that is true" to 5 = "no, that is not true"). Items are scored 1–5, with 10 [fatigue-]positively phrased items reverse scored (items 2, 5, 9, 10, 13, 14, 16, 17, 18, 19). In the final score, high scores represent more fatigue. Total score calculated for 5 subscales: - general fatigue (items 1, 5, 12, 16); - physical fatigue (items 2, 8, 14, 20); - reduced activity (items 3, 6, 10, 17); - reduced motivation (items 4, 9, 15, 18); - mental fatigue (i
citation: Smets E, Garssen B, Bonke Bd, De Haes J (1995). The Multidimensional Fatigue Inventory (MFI): psychometric qualities of an instrument to assess fatigue. *Journal of Psychosomatic Research* 39: 315-25. https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/7636775

name: Patient Health Questionnaire 8-item depression scale (PHQ-8)
description: 8-item scale. Auto-converted from CamCOPS; requires manual review.
citation: Kroenke K, Spitzer RL (2002). The PHQ-9: a new depression and diagnostic severity measure. *Psychiatric Annals* 32: 509–521. https://doi.org/10.3928/0048-5713-20020901-06

name: Patient Health Questionnaire-15 (PHQ-15)
description: 15-item scale. Auto-converted from CamCOPS; requires manual review.
citation: See Kroenke et al. (2002) (http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/11914441) and the instructions (http://www.phqscreeners.com/instructions/instructions.pdf).

name: Psychosocial fActors Relevant to BrAin DISorders in Europe–24 (PARADISE 24)

citation: PARADISE Consortium (2015). PARADISE 24: A Measure to Assess the Impact of Brain Disorders on People's Lives. *PLoS One* 10: e0132410. https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/26147343; article https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0132410; Supplementary File 1 https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0132410.s001; Supplementary File 2 https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0132410.s002.

description: For post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD). History and guide Auto-converted from CamCOPS; requires manual review.
